articles.title: Who is Aparna Yadav: Prateek Yadav’s wife and ‘chhoti bahu’ of UP’s influential Yadav clan in focus
articles.description: Prateek Yadav, son of Mulayam Singh Yadav and stepbrother of Akhilesh Yadav, passed away at 38 in Lucknow. His wife, Aparna Bisht Yadav, a former SP candidate who later joined the BJP, currently serves as vice-chairperson of the State Women Commission. Prateek had previously accused Aparna of damaging family relations.

articles.title: Sonia Gandhi in Hospital: Congress Leader Admitted to Medanta, Gurugram; Read Details

articles.title: A tale of two VVIP diplomatic events
articles.description: West Asia: An opinion piece warns that India’s growing alignment with Israel and the UAE could deepen Gulf tensions, weaken ties with Iran and expose New Delhi to economic and diplomatic risks linked to oil, trade, energy security and the diaspora..

articles.title: 'You Drink Margarita With Rapists': Kash Patel Fires Back At Sen. Holland Over Drinking Allegations
articles.description: A dramatic showdown erupted during a U.S. Senate hearing after Kash Patel and Senator Chris Van Hollen exchanged explosive accusations over alleged alcohol abuse and taxpayer-funded conduct. During a heated FBI budget hearing, Van Hollen confronted Patel over reports claiming the FBI Director was frequently drunk or hungover while on duty. Patel furiously denied the allegations, calling them “a total farce” and accusing the media of spreading fraudulent claims. The hearing escalated further when Patel fired back, accusing Van Hollen of “drinking on the taxpayer’s dime” during a controversial trip to El Salvador. The tense exchange stunned the Senate chamber and ended with both men agreeing to undergo a drinking “audit.”

articles.title: Iran Restores Hormuz Missile Sites During Ceasefire? US Intel Sounds Alarm Over Rapid Recovery
articles.description: Iran restores 30 Hormuz missile sites as US intel contradicts ‘shattered military’ claims. Classified American intelligence assessments reportedly reveal that Iran regained operational access to most missile facilities along the Strait of Hormuz after the ceasefire. The findings challenge repeated claims from Donald Trump’s administration that Tehran’s military capabilities had been destroyed. Reports suggest Iran still retains nearly seventy percent of its missile stockpile, while underground launch facilities across the country are now partially or fully operational. The developments come after months of American-Israeli attacks and Iran’s massive retaliatory missile campaign targeting US and Israeli assets across the region.#Iran #Hormuz #USIntel #Trump #IRGC #MiddleEast #Missiles #PersianGulf #Israel #BreakingNews #Tehran #StraitOfHormuz #USIran #MilitaryNews #Geopolitics

articles.title: Prateek Yadav, Mulayam Singh Yadav's son, dies at 38; leaders offer condolences
articles.description: Prateek Yadav, son of the late Mulayam Singh Yadav and husband of Aparna Yadav, passed away suddenly at 38. Leaders from various political parties, including the Samajwadi Party and BJP, expressed their grief. Deputy Chief Ministers Keshav Prasad Maurya and Brajesh Pathak offered condolences. The Uttar Pradesh Congress chief also mourned the loss.

articles.description: The government has not rolled out any formal policy after the PM's appeal, hence, decisions by ministers and the administration to rein in travel are ad hoc.

articles.title: TVK MLA Srinivasa Sethupathi who won by 1 vote barred from Vijay's floor test; SC to hear case today

The court cited the potential impact of his disputed vote on the state's constitutional governance. The MLA has appealed the order to the Supreme Court.

articles.title: 'People's will through lawmakers can bring religious reforms': Supreme Court
articles.description: Constitutional courts should be extremely reluctant to alter religious practices, a nine-judge Supreme Court bench cautioned. Only the will of the people, expressed through elected representatives in Parliament and assemblies, can bring about reforms in religious customs. The court emphasized that it's impossible to scrutinize every religious practice.

articles.title: Trump Iran War MOCKED In Viral Arcade Game Played By National Guard Troops
articles.description: National Guard troops stationed in Washington D.C. were spotted playing a viral anti-Trump arcade game installed on the National Mall, sparking massive reactions online. The controversial game, Epic Furious: Strait to Hell, mocks President Donald Trump, the Iran conflict, and top administration officials through dark satire and impossible-to-win gameplay. Photos and videos of troops interacting with the machines near the Capitol and Washington Monument are now spreading rapidly across social media.

He added that listeners should understand art in a broader way instead of generalising an entire genre based on a few songs.

articles.title: 10 Drowning Street? UK PM Keir Starmer labours on amid strong current
articles.description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer has rejected calls to resign following a significant election defeat, stating he will continue to govern despite internal party pressure. While acknowledging responsibility, Starmer emphasized that no formal leadership challenge has been initiated, with loyal ministers expressing their support. The situation leaves Starmer and party rebels in a stalemate as the country awaits his next move.

articles.title: Putin's Forces Inflict Crushing Defeat On Zelensky's Troops, Over 8,000 Troops Killed In 7 Days
articles.description: Russia has claimed that Ukrainian forces suffered massive losses during one of the war’s most intense weeks of fighting. According to Russian military expert Andrey Marochko, citing figures from Russia’s Defense Ministry, more than 8,400 Ukrainian troops and foreign mercenaries were killed or wounded between May 2 and May 8. Moscow also claims its forces destroyed hundreds of military assets, including over 700 combat vehicles, 77 artillery systems, multiple rocket launchers, electronic warfare systems, and nearly 4,300 drones. The heaviest clashes were reportedly concentrated in the Dnipro and Zaporizhzhia regions.
